No Poverty.reason: Contract law, while not directly addressing poverty, is foundational for economic participation and securing rights to resources (Target 1.4), indirectly supporting poverty reduction efforts. Both models converged on this weak link.

Zero Hunger.reason: No evidence in the course description or competencies directly or indirectly links to ending hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No evidence in the course description or competencies directly or indirectly links to health, well-being, or related services.

Quality Education.reason: As a university-level seminar, the course directly provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops professional knowledge, practical application, and critical analysis skills (Targets 4.4, 4.7).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or competencies links to gender equality or related issues.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence in the course description or competencies directly or indirectly links to clean water, sanitation, or related environmental issues.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence in the course description or competencies directly or indirectly links to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Contract law is fundamental for economic activity, entrepreneurship (Target 8.3), and protecting labor rights (Target 8.8). The course develops 'professional knowledge' essential for these areas.
evidence: 專業知識與實務運用的能力

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Contract law provides the essential legal framework for industrial operations and infrastructure development (Target 9.1). This foundational role warrants a low, inferred score.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The 'humane care' competency and the general role of a just legal system, including contract law, in ensuring fair treatment and equal opportunity (Target 10.3) provide a weak but valid link.

Life Below Water.reason: No evidence in the course description or competencies directly or indirectly links to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence in the course description or competencies directly or indirectly links to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: A 'Contract Law Seminar' directly supports the rule of law and ensures equal access to justice (Target 16.3), which are fundamental to building strong, accountable institutions (Target 16.6).
